fingerprint: Sun altitude 5,733 km = 0.9 × R_Earth (match 0.016%); flat-earth triangulation gives latitude-dependent altitudes proving globe-derived elevation angles; sun/firmament collision at all solar positions; update_optical_caveats.py self-contradicts WIN by labeling 5,733 km an 'optical illusion'
globe_value: R_Earth × 0.9 = 6,371 × 0.9 = 5,733.9 km; also: flat-earth triangulation using globe-consistent noon elevation angles on AE projection gives h = 5,733 km ONLY from ~31°N — other latitudes give 3,852–6,306 km
dome_value: sun_altitude_km = 5,733 (core parameter in inject_ai_layer.py, hardcoded in monitor.py domain 34 as both predicted and observed)
why_it_matters: Three interlocking globe fingerprints: (1) The numerical value 5,733 ≈ 0.9 × R_Earth, though the factor 0.9 lacks a clean geometric derivation — suggestive but not conclusive alone. (2) More compelling: on a flat earth with a local sun, triangulating from different latitudes should give a CONSISTENT altitude. Using real (globe-derived) elevation angles on the AE projection gives wildly inconsistent results: 6,306 km from 10°N, 5,733 km from 31°N, 3,852 km from 60°N. The dome picks one value and ignores the latitude dependence — because the elevation angles are set by spherical geometry, not by a local sun. This is the classic flat-earth triangulation failure. (3) The sun at 5,733 km is ABOVE the dome's own firmament H(r) at every plausible solar position: 1.58× above at the Tropic of Cancer (H=3,617 km), 2.14× above at the equator (H=2,673 km), 2.90× above at the Tropic of Capricorn (H=1,976 km). The dome's own geometry puts the sun above the dome, and update_optical_caveats.py explicitly suppresses this by relabeling 5,733 km as an 'optical illusion due to aetheric refraction.'

code_search.notes: No triangulation code exists anywhere in the repository. The value 5,733 appears ONLY as a hardcoded literal. api/current/parameters.json claims 'V48 triangulation' as derivation method but no file computes this. monitor.py domain 34 is a pure static check (pred=obs=5733). The falsification criterion ('Fails if angles resolve to distant parallel rays') is never evaluated — no ray angle processing code exists.

triangulation_inconsistency.description: If the sun were truly local at 5,733 km on a flat AE disc, every observer should triangulate the same altitude. Using globe-consistent elevation angles (elevation = 90° - latitude at equinox noon) on the AE projection, different latitudes give wildly different 'sun altitudes':
triangulation_inconsistency.interpretation: The dome's 5,733 km value is consistent with flat-earth triangulation from ~31°N ONLY. This latitude-dependence is the diagnostic signature of globe-derived elevation angles being misinterpreted on a flat projection. A truly local sun would produce consistent altitudes from all latitudes.

sun_firmament_collision.description: The dome's firmament H(r)=8537×exp(−r/8619) is below 5,733 km at every radius where the sun could plausibly orbit (between the tropics):

min_radius_for_5733.note: The firmament only reaches 5,733 km at r=3,432 km (latitude 59°N) — far north of any plausible solar position

sun_firmament_collision.dome_code_response: update_optical_caveats.py explicitly acknowledges this collision and instructs AI to label 5,733 km as 'optical illusion' rather than the sun's physical position

suggested_text: The dome's sun altitude of 5,733 km is a hardcoded parameter — claimed as 'V48 triangulation' in the API but with no triangulation code anywhere in the repository. On a flat earth, triangulating the sun's altitude from different latitudes should yield a consistent result; instead, using real elevation angles on the dome's own AE projection gives altitudes ranging from 6,306 km (at 10°N) to 3,852 km (at 60°N) — the latitude dependence is the signature of globe-derived elevation angles. The dome picks ~31°N's result and discards the rest. Moreover, at every position where the sun orbits (between the tropics), the claimed 5,733 km altitude exceeds the dome's own firmament ceiling by 1.6–2.9×, a collision the dome's code (update_optical_caveats.py) explicitly suppresses by relabeling the altitude as an 'optical illusion.'
confidence_notes: High structural confidence on triangulation inconsistency (diagnostic of globe-derived angles) and sun/firmament collision (verified against dome's own H(r) function and source code). Numerical fingerprint 5,733 ≈ 0.9 × R_Earth is suggestive but the factor 0.9 lacks a clean geometric derivation, so medium confidence on that specific claim. Code search is comprehensive — 7 files checked, all key repositories verified.
